title: Thlaspi lerescheanum
intro: Thlaspi lerescheanum, also known as Leresche's penny-cress, is a perennial plant species within the Brassicaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support immune function and may help in reducing cardiovascular disease risk. The bioactive constituents of Thlaspi lerescheanum include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. Thlaspi lerescheanum is typically used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Thlaspi lerescheanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a member of the genus Thlaspi, which includes various species of cress and pennycress. The Brassicaceae family consists of plants commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. The classification of Thlaspi lerescheanum is further divided into various ranks, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species. This classification helps in identifying and understanding the plant's characteristics and relationships with other species.
